summary: Bridge MAX_CONCURRENT_TRADES cap and per-intent re-fetch protection verified and operational

initial_cap_check.action: logs BRIDGE SKIP and skips entire bridge block

per_intent_refetch.purpose: Catches concurrent writes from other processes

cap_hit_during_loop.action: logs BRIDGE CAP HIT and breaks loop

race_condition_protection.action: logs BRIDGE RACE SKIP for concurrently opened slug

acceptance_criteria_verification: ✓ MAX_CONCURRENT_TRADES cap enforced before bridge processing
✓ Per-intent re-fetch catches concurrent writes from other processes
✓ Race condition where slug opens between outer fetch and per-intent fetch is properly handled
✓ Bridge logs BRIDGE SKIP when at capacity before loop starts
✓ Bridge logs BRIDGE CAP HIT and breaks if capacity reached during loop
✓ Bridge logs BRIDGE RACE SKIP for concurrently opened slugs

implementation_notes.root_cause_addressed: 2026-04-04 incident: 6 concurrent bot_core.py processes each opened trades for same slugs, causing -$218 losses. Now prevented by MAX_CONCURRENT_TRADES cap + per-intent re-fetch
implementation_notes.bridge_status: Currently disabled (ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE=False per kill switch). This fix is a prerequisite for re-enabling.
implementation_notes.duplicate_slug_protection: Additional _bridge_consumed_slugs deduplication logic also present (lines 532-550), implemented by BRIDGE_ENTRY_GATE_DUPE_SLUG_FIX_001

notes: This task was marked DONE by SONNET_MANAGER. Verification confirms the bridge section now enforces MAX_CONCURRENT_TRADES cap at loop entry and re-fetches open positions before each intent processing to catch race conditions from concurrent processes. The fix prevents the 2026-04-04 incident pattern from recurring.
